steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that peaking plants can generate more MWh/yr than some run-of-river plants. The example must show a specific peaking plant outperforming at least one run-of-river plant in annual power generation using data from the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘average power generated annually by the J. Woodruff plant is higher than that generated by any of the run-of-river plants in the table.’: J. Woodruff is a peaking plant. The table shows its MWh/yr exceeds every run-of-river plant listed — this directly illustrates the claim with quantitative data. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘J. Woodruff plant, which is a peaking plant, has more generators than any of the other plants in the table.’: Generator count is not the same as power output (MWh/yr). This does not illustrate the claim about annual energy generation. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘average power generated annually by the Norway plant is higher than that generated by the Superior Falls plant.’: Both Norway and Superior Falls are run-of-river plants. Comparing two run-of-river plants does not illustrate the peaking-vs.-run-of-river contrast the claim makes. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘run-of-river plant with the highest average annual power generation in the table generated more electricity than the peaking plant with the highest annual power generation in the table.’: This inverts the relationship stated in the claim and contradicts the table data. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
